title: Lesson 358
transcript: One small but important topic that we need to talk about here related to cloud security is cloud security roles. And there's really, for the CISSP exam, there's really only two major roles that you need to know about that I've actually been mentioning, but let's formally define them here. So the first one is the cloud consumer or the cloud customer. This is the individual or organization who's consuming, using the cloud services. And then, of course, the other major one is the cloud service provider, the CSP. This is the organization that's providing the cloud services. And there's two really important words we have to talk about here related to this accountability and responsibility. Who's accountable here? The answer unequivocally is the consumer. The customer is gonna be accountable for any system, any data, any systems that they run in the cloud, and the cloud service provider will be responsible for any customer data, any systems that are running in their cloud. Because remember, of course, you can't outsource accountability. It's a really important concept to understand. But here's a good question. Could a single organization be both the consumer and the provider? The answer is yes, right? Like if an organization was using, say, a private cloud deployment model where they built their own private cloud on-prem in their own data center, they would be both the consumer and the provider of that cloud. So these two roles don't necessarily have to be different organizations. They could potentially be just within one organization. The other roles here, things like cloud service partners and all these other things here, brokers, etc. This is actually a slide from our CCSP deck. So don't worry about memorizing all these for the whatever this is, the CISSP exam. It's getting late in the day here. Okay. And you've already seen this exact slide before, but I just, I really want to kind of beat you over the head with it. Accountability versus responsibility. This is such a critically important concept related to cloud security, right? And the owner remains accountable. And it is impossible to outsource accountability. All the owner can do is outsource responsibility. And this is why we spend so much time talking about contracts and SLAs and everything else because... the owner remains accountable. They have to make sure that they've identified the risks, that they've identified the controls that will mitigate those risks. And then they've communicated, they need to be making sure that the cloud service provider who's responsible is implementing the right controls and that they're operating effectively and so forth, right? So all that the owner can outsource is the responsibility, but not the accountability. So really important to keep that in mind.
